revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 46 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同神とされ、[[天児屋根命]]と共に[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。又、「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。更に、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛治師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛治師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html 岐多志太神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 46 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同神とされ、[[天児屋根命]]と共に[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。又、「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。更に、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛治師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛治師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html 岐多志太神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同一とされ、[[天児屋根命]]とともに[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kitashita Shrine'''|岐多志太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kitashita Shrine'''|岐多志太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Kitashita Shrine'''|岐多志太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kitashita Shrine'''|岐多志太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同一とされ、[[天児屋根命]]とともに[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

[[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|岐多志太神社|version=106919233|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同一とされ、[[天児屋根命]]とともに[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|岐多志太神社|version=106919233|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = [[天香山命]]
[[天児屋根命]] |社格 = [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]（小）
旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[崇神天皇]]7年 |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月6日]] |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[磯城郡]][[田原本町]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]で、旧社格は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。 == 祭神 == 以下の二柱を祀る。 *[[天香山命]] *[[天児屋根命]] 社伝では、[[天香山命]]は[[石凝姥命]]と同一とされ、[[天児屋根命]]とともに[[鏡作坐天照御魂神社]]と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]7年の鎮座とされる。[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[城下郡]]の小社に列せられた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:岐多志太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153279}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}}
{{jalink|天児屋根命}} |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 祭神 === 以下の二柱を祀る。 *{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} 社伝では、{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}}は{{jalink|石凝姥命}}と同一とされ、{{jalink|天児屋根命}}とともに{{jalink|鏡作坐天照御魂神社|Q38278496}}と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 === 歴史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年の鎮座とされる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では{{jalink|城下郡}}の小社に列せられた。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 外部リンク === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|lt=Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} ** Series ordinal: 147 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039002|lt=Q135039002|WD=Q135039002}} ** P156: {{ill|Yamato 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * きたしたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3150005005200 == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q135018062|lt=Q135018062|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県磯城郡田原本町伊与戸143', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県磯城郡田原本町大木', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:岐多志太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153279}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 岐多志太神社 |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}伊与戸143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}}
{{jalink|天児屋根命}} |社格 = {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 祭神 === 以下の二柱を祀る。 *{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} 社伝では、{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}}は{{jalink|石凝姥命}}と同一とされ、{{jalink|天児屋根命}}とともに{{jalink|鏡作坐天照御魂神社|Q38278496}}と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 === 歴史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年の鎮座とされる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では{{jalink|城下郡}}の小社に列せられた。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 外部リンク === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

旧{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月6日|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|社叢]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|拝殿]]}} '''岐多志太神社'''（きたしたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|近代社格制度#諸社|Q712534|村社}}。 === 祭神 === 以下の二柱を祀る。 *{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|天児屋根命}} 社伝では、{{jalink|天香山命|Q10940849}}は{{jalink|石凝姥命}}と同一とされ、{{jalink|天児屋根命}}とともに{{jalink|鏡作坐天照御魂神社|Q38278496}}と同様の祭神を祀っているとされる。「岐多志太」の社号は「キタ氏の田」という意味であり、キタ（鉄）を鍛える鉄工の神であるという。そして、この地域を根拠地とした物部大木連の一族に、鏡作連の祖である'''鍛冶師連'''（きたしのむらじ）があり、この鍛冶師連が奉斎した神社が当社とされる。 === 歴史 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}7年の鎮座とされる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では{{jalink|城下郡}}の小社に列せられた。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 外部リンク === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} enshrines the same deities as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:岐多志太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153279}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kitashita Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} enshrines the same deities as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 6|Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} enshrines the same deities as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} enshrines the same deities as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:岐多志太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153279}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kitashita Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kitashita Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} enshrines the same deities as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496|WD=Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai Shōsha}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}} enshrines the same deities as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496|WD=Q38278496}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} enshrines the same deities as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496|WD=Q38278496|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたしたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Iyodo 143 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 33 | 緯度秒 = 2.7 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 56.0 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} enshrines the same deities as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496|WD=Q38278496|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#miscellaneous shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 6|Q2928|WD=Q2928|1=October 6}} |神事 = }} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Shasou.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Shrine grove]]}} {{右|[[画像:Kitashita Jinja Haiden.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Worship hall]]}} '''Kitashita Shrine''' (きたしたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deities === The following two deities are enshrined. *{{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Ame no Kaguyama no Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} is identified with Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto, and together with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} enshrines the same deities as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri niimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|Q38278496|WD=Q38278496|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine}}. The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

The shrine name "Kitashita" means "the field of the Kita clan," and is interpreted as a deity of metalworkers who forged "kita" (iron). Among the Mononobe Ōki no Muraji clan based in this region, there was the '''Kitashi no Muraji''' (forging lineage), ancestors of the Kagamitsukuri no Muraji, and this shrine is said to have been the one they ritually served. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a minor shrine of Jōge District. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/yamato/html/031205-01.html |title=岐多志太神社 |date=20181001131139}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitashita Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
